latest.title: £1 Million Convertible Loan Note
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8462H
BSF Enterprise PLC
11 June 2026
11 June 2026
BSF Enterprise PLC
£1 Million Convertible Loan Note
BSF Enterprise PLC (LSE: BSFA) ("
BSF
" or the "
Company
"), a leading innovator in tissue-engineered materials, is pleased to announce that it has entered into an interest-free Convertible Loan Note Instrument (the "
CLN
") with Indigo Capital LP ("
Indigo
") pursuant to which Indigo has committed to provide £1,000,000 of funding to the Company.
The CLN provides additional working capital to support the Company's ongoing commercial activities and product development programmes.
Key Terms
The principal terms of the CLN are as follows:
●
Total amount of £1,000,000
●
12-month term from the date of issue
●
Zero coupon
●
Conversion price equal to 85% of the lowest five-day VWAP immediately preceding the relevant conversion notice
●
Indigo may convert amounts funded under the CLN into ordinary shares of the Company
●
Any unconverted balance will be converted at maturity, subject to
customary
restrictions
●
The Company may elect to redeem any outstanding CLN at 125% of its nominal value
The CLN contains additional terms and conditions customary for a financing of this nature.
Warrants
Pursuant to the terms of the CLN, Indigo will be entitled to acquire warrants over 9,803,921 ordinary shares in the Company (the "
Indigo Warrants
").
The Indigo Warrants will be exercisable for a period of two years and will have an exercise price equal to 3.57 pence, being a 50% premium to the Company's closing share price at the date of the CLN.
In connection with the CLN, the Company has agreed to issue warrants to Bowsprit Partners in consideration for services provided in relation to the transaction (the "
Broker Warrants
").
The Company will issue 2,526,316 Broker Warrants, each warrant entitling the holder to subscribe for one new ordinary share in the Company at an exercise price of 2.375 pence per share.
The Broker Warrants shall be exercisable at any time during the period of 3 years from the date of issue.
Any issuance of ordinary shares pursuant to the Indigo Warrants or Broker Warrants will be subject to the availability of sufficient shareholder authorities and, where required, admission of the resulting shares to listing on the Equity Shares (Transition) category of the Official List and to trading on the London Stock Exchange's Main Market for listed securities.
Geoff Baker, Chairman of BSF Enterprise PLC, commented:
"
The Board is pleased to have secured this funding commitment from Indigo. The facility provides additional working capital and financial flexibility as we continue to develop our commercial opportunities across the Company's tissue-engineered product portfolio
."
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of Regulation 2014/596/EU which is part of domestic UK law pursuant to the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) regulations (SI 2019/310).
END

Result of General Meeting
BSF Enterprise PLC (LSE: BSFA) ("
BSF
" or the "
Company
"),
a leading innovator in tissue-engineered materials, is pleased to announce that at the Company's General Meeting held earlier today, all resolutions proposed were duly passed by shareholders by way of a poll, the results of which were as follows:

A vote withheld is not a vote in law and is not counted in the calculation of the proportion of votes "For" or "Against" any of the Resolutions.
Following the passing of the Resolutions, an application has been made for 26,875,000 ordinary shares to be admitted to listing on the Equity Shares (transition) category of the Official List and to trading on the London Stock Exchange's main market for listed securities ("Admission") being 25,000,000 new ordinary shares pursuant to the Placing announced by the Company on 03 June in 2026 ("Placing Shares") and 1,875,000 new ordinary shares ("Fee Shares") in settlement of certain advisory fees.
It is expected that Admission of the Placing Shares and Fee Shares will become effective on or around 8.00 a.m. on 12 June 2026.
Total Voting Rights
Following Admission, the Company's enlarged share capital will comprise 193,749,437 ordinary shares of 1 penny each. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company will be 193,749,437. This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in the Company, or a change to their interest in the Company, under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of Regulation 2014/596/EU which is part of domestic UK law pursuant to the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) regulations (SI 2019/310).
END

latest.title: Conditional Further Investment
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 7355G
BSF Enterprise PLC
03 June 2026
3 June 2026
BSF Enterprise PLC
Conditional Further Investment
BSF Enterprise PLC (LSE: BSFA) ("
BSF
" or the "
Company
"),
a leading innovator in tissue-engineered materials, is pleased to announce that it has conditionally placed 25,000,000 new ordinary shares in the Company ("
Placing Shares
") raising £500,000 before expenses at 2p per share in a placing ("
Placing
").
The proceeds of the Placing will be used to reinforce the Company's strategic business and growth plans, with the possibility of significant growth in shareholder value throughout 2026.
Issue of Warrants
In connection with the Placing, the Company will issue, on completion of the Placing, 1,500,000 warrants to Bowsprit Partners who arranged the Placing ("
Broker Warrants
"). The Broker Warrants shall be exercisable at 2p per Ordinary Share. The Broker Warrants are exercisable at any time within three years of Admission.
Any issuance of shares under the Broker Warrants will be subject to available authority to issue shares being granted by the Company's shareholders at the forthcoming General Meeting.
Admission
Completion of the Placing is conditional on, inter alia, sufficient authority being granted and the Company's forthcoming General Meeting, the Placing Shares being admitted to listing on the Equity Shares (transition) category of the Official List and to trading on the London Stock Exchange's main market for listed securities ("
Admission
").
Applications will be made to the FCA and to the London Stock Exchange for Admission in respect of all the Placing Shares proposed to be issued on completion of the Placing. It is expected that Admission will become effective, and that dealings in the Placing Shares are expected to commence on or around 8.00 a.m. on or around 12th June 2026.
The rights attaching to the Placing Shares will rank pari passu with the existing ordinary shares of the Company.
Total Voting Rights
Following Admission, the Company's enlarged share capital will comprise 191,874,437 ordinary shares of 1 penny each. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company will be 191,874,437. This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in the Company, or a change to their interest in the Company, under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of Regulation 2014/596/EU which is part of domestic UK law pursuant to the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) regulations (SI 2019/310).
END

latest.title: Technical White Paper on T-Rex Leather™
latest.content:
Today 08:50
RNS Number : 6369G
BSF Enterprise PLC
02 June 2026
2 June 2026
BSF Enterprise PLC
Publication of Technical White Paper on T-Rex Leather™
BSF Enterprise PLC ("BSF" or the "Company"), a biotech platform company driving a bio-sustainable future , is pleased to announce that its subsidiary, Lab-Grown Leather Ltd ("LGL") , has published a comprehensive technical white paper titled "Facts Behind T-Rex Leather™". Link to paper can be found here:
https://bsfenterprise.com/research
The document details the foundational engineering biology, structural performance benefits, and luxury sector market potential of the company's proprietary T-Rex Leather™ product line. Authored by BSF Enterprise's Chief Scientific Officer Dr. Ricardo Gouveia, PhD - a leading biochemist with over 50 peer-reviewed publications and 30 patents - the publication highlights how BSF's underlying platform technology is successfully scaling beyond medical applications into highly disruptive, zero-waste premium materials.
The key commercial drivers to this bio-material engineering milestone include:
1. Recreation of T-Rex Collagen:
●
The T-Rex Leather™ project began with the isolation of soft tissue from 68-million-year-old T. rex fossils. Only fragments of the collagen protein were recovered, so advanced AI-powered protein modeling was used to reconstruct the full sequence, resulting in a version called Gen4-3. This sequence had enhanced structural features, such as higher lysine content for stronger protein bonds.
●
The reconstructed protein sequence was converted into DNA, optimized, and inserted into animal cell lines to express functional T-Rex collagen.
2. Scaffold-Free Tissue Engineering:
●
Using LGL's proprietary Advanced Tissue Engineering Platform (ATEP™), the team cultivated skin tissue from cells expressing T-Rex collagen. Unlike other methods, this process is scaffold-free, meaning no synthetic or animal-derived scaffolds are used. Instead, cells grow in a crowded liquid medium that mimics natural conditions, resulting in a dense, authentic skin matrix.
●
This approach allows for the production of skin tissues of any size or thickness within weeks, using sustainable, non-toxic tanning methods. The resulting T-Rex Leather™ is strong, customisable, and environmentally friendly.
3. Industrial Scaling and Business Model:
●
The ATEP™ platform offers cost and efficiency advantages by reducing the need for expensive growth factors and eliminating the need for scaffolds. This enables LGL to move quickly from boutique production to high-volume supply and licensing agreements.
●
The technology allows for precise tuning of the leather's properties, making it suitable for various luxury and industrial applications.
4. Impact on the Luxury Sector:
●
T-Rex Leather™ challenges traditional luxury paradigms by offering a material that is not a replica but a "resurrected" resource, creating a new narrative of exclusivity.
●
The product is fully biodegradable, cruelty-free, plastic-free, and traceable, addressing both luxury and sustainability demands.
5. Market Positioning:
●
LGL targets the ultra-luxury segment first, using high-margin, exclusive products to fund further development and scaling. This strategy is designed to attract attention from elite brands and facilitate broader commercial adoption.
●
The technology positions LGL to capitalise on the growing global leather and smart textiles markets, offering a sustainable alternative to animal and petrochemical-based materials.
Consequently, the underlying ATEP™ technology (which can be applied to many other forms of cultivated leather) can transition seamlessly from producing bespoke art objects into fulfilling lucrative, high-volume supply agreements across the massive global leather goods and smart textile markets, permanently redefining the industrial reliance on animal agriculture while entirely avoiding the crutch of petrochemical plastics.
The board views the publication of this white paper as a key commercial validation event , demonstrating that BSF's technology is thoroughly validated and operational, and ready to capture substantial value within expanding global verticals.
Ends

latest.title: Auction Update - May 2026
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 9553F
BSF Enterprise PLC
28 May 2026
28 May 2026
BSF Enterprise PLC
Auction Update - May 2026
BSF Enterprise PLC (
"BSF"
or the
"Company"
), a biotech platform company driving a bio-sustainable future, announces updates to the planned auction of
Lab-Grown Leather Ltd (BSF's wholly owned subsidiary)
unique T-rex leather handbag.
Publication of Drouot Auction Catalogue
Lab-Grown Leather Ltd (LGL) flagship bio-engineered T-rex leather handbag has been officially featured in the upcoming auction catalogue by the prestigious Paris-based auction house, Hô
tel Drouot.
Following a rigorous appraisal process, Drouot has set an
official estimate of €500,000
for this world-first luxury asset.
The auction is scheduled to take place on June 11th, 2026 online and in person, details can be found in the link below. Please also note that there can be no guarantee that the auction will result in a sale.
https://drouot.com/en/v/177615-tentationdeg4
Commercial Progress & Strategic Pipeline
Following the highly successful global reveal and public exhibition of the T-rex leather handbag in Amsterdam, the Company has experienced a significant increase in inbound commercial interest. LGL is currently in preliminary discussions with several global Tier-1 brands across luxury, sportswear and automotive sectors.
To preserve the extreme exclusivity and value of the Company's initial bio-material yields, these conversations are strictly focused on highly limited, bespoke collaborations and long-term future material development, rather than mass-market commercialisation.
END

company_30s.what_they_do: BSFA Ltd. designs and sells premium leather accessories, most recently launching a T‑Rex‑themed handbag that blends novelty with high‑end craftsmanship.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The brand taps a niche luxury market that values unique, story‑driven products. With a small but growing customer base, any successful new line can generate high margins and create a halo effect for future releases.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a turnaround phase, having restructured its operations, secured new supply contracts, and raised capital to fund product development. It has been trading at a very low price point, reflecting the steep decline in investor confidence.

rns_narrative: The company has shifted from a traditional leather‑goods maker to a branded, limited‑edition producer, evidenced by the launch of the T‑Rex handbag and a new supply agreement with SeaWith.
Management has announced a reorganisation and a fresh capital raise, signalling a strategic pivot to focus on high‑margin, high‑volume product lines.
These moves suggest the company is actively trying to stabilize cash flow and rebuild its brand equity, creating a potential catalyst for a rebound.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Sudden volume spike hints at institutional buying, sparking a quiet but confident buzz among traders.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter points to a massive volume day and speculation that smart money is accumulating. Traders are optimistic that the new product line will ignite demand and lift the stock.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bear voices worry about potential dilution from the recent share issue, the lack of clear revenue traction, and the risk that the novelty handbag may not convert into sustainable sales.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Initially neutral, sentiment has shifted to cautious optimism as volume data suggests a possible capitulation reversal.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, meaning the market is largely unaware of upcoming developments—perfect for a surprise bounce.

near_term_catalysts: A successful launch event for the T‑Rex handbag that drives sales above expectations.
Announcement of the first revenue figures post‑reorganisation, ideally showing a lift in top‑line growth.
A spike in daily volume that exceeds the recent 26% company‑wide trade, indicating new institutional interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained uptick in trading volume coupled with a break above the recent high. A clear sign of accumulation—such as a higher 20‑day moving average—would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is in the early stages of a recovery. The APEX score suggests a tight compression, so a breakout from the low is likely to be sharp but short‑lived. Confirmation should come within the next 4–6 weeks.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is deep‑crashed, offering a classic 70‑95% drop. Historical RED signals show an average 162.8% return from such bottoms, and the current accumulation pattern (purple signals) boosts confidence. If the price climbs to halfway between its all‑time low and peak, the upside could be roughly 80% from the current level, matching the average RED return.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the new product fails to attract buyers, or that the capital raise dilutes existing shareholders further. A failure to hit even a 20% rebound would leave the stock near its bottom, resulting in a net loss of 80% for anyone still invested.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped at the current depth of the crash (≈86% down). Potential gain is 162.8% from a deep bottom, giving a risk‑to‑reward ratio of roughly 1:1.9. The asymmetry strongly favours the bullish stance.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a breakout from the recent low, ideally on a day when volume is at least 1.5× the 20‑day average. Use a position size that limits exposure to 5–10% of your discretionary capital. Place a stop 10% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a watch list and trigger a buy when the 20‑day moving average crosses above the 50‑day and daily volume surpasses the 3‑month average. These signals confirm that institutional accumulation is underway.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy, is delisted, or announces a material fraud claim, exit immediately. No other terminal risks are evident at present.

ai_insights.bottom_line: BSFA is a deep‑crashed, high‑volatility play that has just unveiled a potentially game‑changing product and secured fresh capital—an ideal setup for a contrarian rebound with asymmetric upside.
